summary: Iter_16: Both delete flows verified end-to-end. BACKEND: 9/9 pytest tests pass in /app/backend/tests/test_delete_flows_iter16.py — DELETE /api/admin/employees/{user_id} correctly (a) 200 for super_admin with cascade dict {attendance,leaves,tickets,payslips,notifications,user_sessions}, (b) 401 unauth, (c) 403 with invalid token, (d) 404 unknown user, (e) 403 when target is a super_admin (safety guard). DELETE /api/companies/{company_id} correctly (a) 200 with empty cascade when no linked users, (b) 409 with detail containing 'still linked' when users exist and force!=true, (c) 200 with cascade counts (users>=1) when force=true, (d) 404 unknown company, (e) 401 unauth. FRONTEND E2E: signed in as super admin, changed PIN, navigated to /admin — trash icon testID=`delete-emp-<user_id>` was visible next to seeded employee (+919999123456 in TEST_Iter16_FE_Co), tapping it fired ONE window.confirm and the row was removed from the list on next load. Navigated to /companies — deleting a company WITH employees fired TWO confirms (initial 'Delete X?' then 'FORCE DELETE ... will permanently remove ALL employees...' quoting backend message '1 employee(s) still linked'), and the company card disappeared; deleting a company WITHOUT employees fired ONLY ONE confirm and disappeared. CLEANUP: all test companies (TEST_Iter16_FE_Co, TEST_Iter16_WithEmps, TEST_Iter16_Empty and pytest-created ones) deleted, verified `GET /companies` returns []; super admin state reset to {pin_hash=bcrypt('246810'), pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null} and final POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login → 200 with pin_must_change=true. Note: companies.tsx trash button had no testID; I added testID=`delete-company-<id>` to enable stable E2E test (minor testable-attribute addition, no logic change).

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing deprecation warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — carried over, not blocking.
Pre-existing: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — carried over, not blocking.

action_items: No pending action items — both fixes (Delete Employee endpoint + Delete Company force cascade) are verified working end-to-end.
OPTIONAL (carried): server.py is 2366 lines; splitting into auth/companies/attendance modules is still recommended (well above 700-line guideline).

OPTIONAL: consider a company_admin scenario test — my role-scope check only used an invalid token (401), not a real company_admin session, because seeding a fresh company_admin PIN requires promotion. Server code path is present and covered by super_admin guard tests; add a dedicated fixture in a future iteration if desired.
critical_code_review_comments: DELETE /api/admin/employees/{user_id} (server.py:1821) — check order is: role→lookup→super_admin_target→company_scope→self. Note: for a super_admin logging in and passing their own user_id, the 'super_admin cannot be deleted' guard fires BEFORE the self-delete guard, so 403 (not 400) is returned. This is safe and consistent with intent, but the docstring 'trying to delete self → 400' in the review request will only trip for company_admin self-delete (not super_admin self-delete). Documented, no fix needed.

DELETE /api/companies/{company_id} (server.py:1780) — response.detail for 409 contains the exact substring 'still linked', matching the frontend heuristic in companies.tsx:196 (`msg.includes('still linked')`). Contract is stable; if the wording is ever changed, the two-step confirm on frontend will silently break — consider returning a structured code (e.g. {error_code:'employees_linked'}) instead of relying on substring match.

companies.tsx trash icon lacked a testID (line 297 before my change); added `delete-company-<id>` for stable E2E. Consider similarly wrapping the 'Force delete' path in a structured response check on frontend to avoid string coupling.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/companies.tsx (added testID=`delete-company-<company_id>` to the per-row trash Pressable — testability only)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (employee delete + company delete with force + empty company delete all pass)

seed_data_creation: During tests created: (a) TEST_Iter16_FE_Co with employee +919999123456 (deleted via UI trash), (b) TEST_Iter16_WithEmps with employee +919999888777 (deleted via UI force-cascade), (c) TEST_Iter16_Empty (deleted via UI), plus multiple pytest-fixture companies/employees (all deleted in their own teardowns / by cleanup step). Final DB state verified via GET /api/companies → [] and super admin login → 200 with pin_must_change=true.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both delete flows (employee + company force cascade) are stable. Regression baseline lives in /app/backend/tests/test_delete_flows_iter16.py — reuse the fixtures for any future employee/company CRUD tests. Note that /api/auth/me returns a NESTED user object ({user: {...}}), not flat — my earlier draft assumed flat and had to be patched. Also companies.tsx now has testID `delete-company-<company_id>` in addition to the existing `company-<company_id>` and `share-code-<company_id>`. Super admin credentials remain sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / 246810 with pin_must_change=true (first login lands on /pin-change).
rca_of_issue: No new bugs. The original user complaint 'Delete Company and Delete Option Not working' had two root causes, both correctly addressed by the main agent: (1) There was no DELETE endpoint for employees at all — now added at server.py:1821 with role-scoped guards and cascade over 6 child collections. (2) DELETE /api/companies rejected any company with linked users and had no override — now the endpoint returns 409 with detail containing the substring 'still linked' when force!=true, and cascade-deletes users+attendance+leaves+tickets+payslips+notifications when ?force=true. Frontend admin.tsx wires a testID=`delete-emp-<user_id>` trash Pressable to a single-confirm doDeleteEmp, and companies.tsx wires a testable trash to doDelete which handles the 409 by popping a second FORCE DELETE confirm and retrying with ?force=true. All four verified via pytest + Playwright.
